Keywords: home homepage product website landing marketing overview brand first impression
Job To Be Done: Establish category fit, value proposition, credibility, and the clearest next action
Required Sections: Hero; Outcome Proof; Feature Grid; How It Works; Social Proof; CTA Band
Primary CTA: Start free, book demo, download app, or explore product
Evidence Required: Product screenshots; customer logos; quantified outcome; clear offer
Internal Links: Feature Overview; Pricing; Use Case; Blog/Resource
Claude Design Prompt Notes: Make the first viewport show the product or real outcome, not generic atmosphere. Keep one primary CTA and one secondary exploration path.
QA Checks: H1 states product/category; nav is scannable; CTA visible on mobile; no unsupported metrics

Keywords: features capabilities product feature overview platform all features
Job To Be Done: Help evaluators understand the product capability map and choose the relevant feature detail path
Required Sections: Page Hero; Feature Grid; Comparison; Outcome Proof; CTA Band
Primary CTA: Explore a feature or start trial
Evidence Required: Screenshots; integration list; capability claims tied to proof
Claude Design Prompt Notes: Organize features by user job, not internal modules. Show how capabilities connect into workflows.
QA Checks: No feature dumping; each feature has user outcome; links to detail pages work

Keywords: feature page feature detail capability workflow automation analytics sleep meditation host tools api
Job To Be Done: Prove one capability solves a specific job better than alternatives
Required Sections: Page Hero; Problem Frame; How It Works; Evidence Block; Related Features; CTA Band
Primary CTA: Use this feature, start trial, or book demo
Evidence Required: Workflow screenshots; before/after state; integration or method proof
Claude Design Prompt Notes: Use a concrete scenario. Include inputs, workflow, output, and failure/edge states.
QA Checks: No invented screenshots; mobile layout preserves sequence; CTA repeats after evidence

Keywords: pricing plans subscription fees packages tiers billing enterprise trial
Job To Be Done: Let buyers compare options, understand value, and take a low-friction next step
Required Sections: Page Hero; Pricing Table; Plan Comparison; Trust Block; FAQ; CTA Band
Primary CTA: Start trial, choose plan, or contact sales
Evidence Required: Plan names; exact prices or contact-sales rule; refund/trial terms; security/payment trust
Claude Design Prompt Notes: Make plan tradeoffs explicit. Avoid hiding critical constraints in small text.

Keywords: use case solution audience industry team role problem workflow customer segment
Job To Be Done: Help a specific audience see their problem, workflow, proof, and next action
Required Sections: Page Hero; Problem Frame; Workflow Steps; Outcome Proof; Relevant Features; CTA Band
Primary CTA: See solution, start trial, or book demo
Evidence Required: Audience-specific pain; measurable outcomes; relevant feature screenshots
Claude Design Prompt Notes: Name the audience and their trigger event. Avoid generic all-user language.

Keywords: customer story case study testimonial proof review success story logo quantified outcome
Job To Be Done: Show believable transformation from problem to measurable result
Required Sections: Page Hero; Problem Frame; Solution Story; Outcome Proof; Related Features; CTA Band
Primary CTA: Read related story or talk to sales
Evidence Required: Customer name or anonymized context; before state; after result; quote approval
Claude Design Prompt Notes: Do not invent logos, quotes, or metrics. Use placeholders when proof is unavailable.

Required Sections: Page Hero; Mission Proof; Team/Principles; Trust Block; CTA Band
Primary CTA: Learn more, contact, or join team

Claude Design Prompt Notes: Keep it specific to why the product exists and how the team makes decisions.

Keywords: trust safety security privacy compliance verification policy marketplace wellness healthcare risk

Primary CTA: Review policy, contact support, or continue signup
Evidence Required: Policies; certifications; moderation process; privacy practices; support SLA
